evals.prompt: Use scrapling to scrape the quotes from https://quotes.toscrape.com/ — extract quote text and author names, print each pair.
evals.expected_output: Agent installs scrapling, imports Fetcher, calls Fetcher().get() with the URL, uses .css() with appropriate selectors to extract quote text and authors from the page, and prints each quote-author pair.
assertions: Fetcher is imported from scrapling.fetchers
Fetcher().get() is called with the URL
.css() is used with a selector that matches quote text elements
At least one quote-author pair is printed
No browser install is required (static fetcher is used)

evals.prompt: Scrape a JavaScript-rendered React SPA at https://example-spa.com/products using scrapling. The product titles load dynamically and appear inside .product-card h2 elements. Wait for the products container to load, then extract all titles.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses DynamicFetcher with wait_selector='.product-card' (or similar), calls .fetch() with the URL and disable_resources=True for speed, then uses .css('.product-card h2').getall() to extract all product titles.
assertions: DynamicFetcher is imported and used instead of Fetcher
fetch() is called (not get())
wait_selector is set to a CSS selector matching loaded content
disable_resources=True is passed for speed
.css() or .xpath() is used to extract titles

evals.prompt: The site https://cf-protected-site.com is behind Cloudflare protection. Use scrapling to bypass it and extract the main article content.
evals.expected_output: Agent installs 'scrapling[fetchers]' and 'cairosvg' (stealth prerequisite), uses StealthyFetcher with headless=True, network_idle=True, and optionally os_randomize=True to fetch the page, then extracts article content with .css('article') or .css('main').
assertions: StealthyFetcher is imported from scrapling.fetchers
StealthyFetcher().fetch() is called with the URL
headless=True is passed
network_idle=True or os_randomize=True is set
Article content is extracted via CSS or XPath selector

evals.prompt: Build a spider using scrapling to crawl https://blog.example.com/, follow pagination links, and extract all blog post titles and dates. The spider should pause and resume if interrupted.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates a Spider subclass with start_urls, implements parse() method that yields title/date dicts and follows pagination via Request objects, sets crawldir for pause/resume, and runs with asyncio.run(spider.start()).
assertions: Spider, Request, Response are imported from scrapling.spiders
A Spider subclass is defined with start_urls
parse() method yields title and date dicts
Pagination link is followed via yield Request(url=..., callback=self.parse)
crawldir is set to a local directory
asyncio.run(spider.start()) or spider.stream() is used to run the crawl
